name: Gravel ride to Caroche peak and the Júcar river
region_intro: Gravel biking around La Canal de Navarrés explores a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, prominent peaks like Caroche, and river valleys such as the Júcar. The region features a network of trails that traverse varied terrain, including areas around reservoirs and historical sites. This environment provides challenging gravel biking experiences with notable climbs and descents. Best gravel bike trails around La Canal de Navarrés The most popular gravel bike trail is Circular gravel from Quesa to Caroche Peak, a 54.0 miles (86.9 km) trail that takes about 7 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain as it circles Caroche Peak. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Gravel ride to Caroche peak and the Júcar river, a difficult 83.6 miles (134.6 km) path. This route combines the challenge of Caroche Peak with sections along the Júcar river. Local gravel bikers also love the Fuente de Marzo – Plaza de La Seo loop from Xàtiva, a 63.3 miles (101.9 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 9 hours 39 minutes. Gravel biking around La Canal de Navarrés is defined by challenging climbs to peaks, descents into river valleys, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore La Canal de Navarrés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in La Canal de Navarrés?
A: La Canal de Navarrés offers over 30 gravel bike trails. These routes are primarily designed for experienced riders, with 9 classified as moderate and 26 as difficult, reflecting the region's challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly gravel bike routes in La Canal de Navarrés?
A: The gravel biking routes in La Canal de Navarrés are generally challenging, characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. There are no routes specifically classified as easy; most are moderate to difficult, making them more suitable for experienced gravel bikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in La Canal de Navarrés?
A: You can expect a diverse and challenging landscape. Trails often feature significant elevation changes, traversing prominent peaks like Caroche and descending into river valleys such as the Júcar. Many routes also explore areas around reservoirs and historical sites, offering varied surfaces and scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in the region?
A: Yes, many routes in La Canal de Navarrés are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1502541253/circular-gravel-from-quesa-to-caroche-peak" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel from Quesa to Caroche Peak&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that loops around the impressive Caroche Peak.

Q: What are some challenging gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668133715/gravel-ride-to-caroche-peak-and-the-jucar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel ride to Caroche peak and the Júcar river&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial climbs and descents over 83 miles. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31497894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mortirolo of Quesa – El Caroche Summit loop from Quesa&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 2,200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in La Canal de Navarrés?
A: The best time for gravel biking in La Canal de Navarrés is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are generally mild, some higher elevation areas might experience cooler conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any interesting natural attractions or landmarks along the gravel bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning gorges and waterfalls, such as the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by reservoirs and offer views of prominent peaks.

Q: Can I find wild swimming spots near the gravel bike trails?
A: The region is known for its natural water features. Highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt; are popular spots for enjoying the water, often accessible from or near various trails. Always exercise caution and respect local regulations when swimming in natural areas.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about La Canal de Navarrés?
A: The komoot community rates the gravel biking experience in La Canal de Navarrés highly, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Riders often praise the challenging climbs, the diverse landscapes encompassing peaks, river valleys, and reservoirs, and the sense of adventure on these demanding routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesa Castle – Public washhouse and Sants River loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Montesa Castle, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: How long do the gravel bike trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1502541253/circular-gravel-from-quesa-to-caroche-peak" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel from Quesa to Caroche Peak&lt;/a&gt; can take around 7 hours, while longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668133715/gravel-ride-to-caroche-peak-and-the-jucar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel ride to Caroche peak and the Júcar river&lt;/a&gt; might require over 14 hours to complete.
tips: The road to the port is paved and is about four kilometers long with very gentle slopes and easy to cycle. From the top you get amazing views.

The last kilometers of the ascent to the Caroig peak on the south face of the mountain are very technical due to the loose stone on the path. However, the scenery you go through and the views you get are second to none.

The Caroig peak is located 1125 meters above sea level. I invite you to make a stop along the way and contemplate the privileged views that you get from this point.

Here you can find the forest hut and information panels about the surrounding peaks.
Beautiful and picturesque site on the road. You can see the Alto del Cinto de la Cabra, the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley and with luck some animal from the area such as deer and foxes in search of food.
Section of road in good condition with beautiful views of the Rambla de Sacaras and the Cortes Reservoir.

This is the epic mountain road along the cliff. It is in very good condition and hardly any cars pass by.

Right at this point, there is a parking lot where you can leave your bike and go up the narrow path, if you want to visit the castle. The views of the reservoir from the top are very beautiful.
From the bridge, you get the best views of Júcar. The contrast of the bluish color of the water and the green of the mountain make it a magical place.

waypoints.name: Pico Caroig Summit and Lookout Tower
